title: prunella × surrecta
intro: Prunella × surrecta, also known as self-heal, is a perennial flowering plant in the mint family that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant contains flavonoids, glycosides, and other compounds with medicinal properties that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Self-heal has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm inflammation. Herbal preparations of self-heal include teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves that can be used topically or internally to harness its health benefits.
